capability_objective: For a read-only operations check, Search separately for “Museum Partnership Briefing” in Seattle and “Fall Campaign Retrospective” in Portland, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete meeting records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. In the final answer, list the two records alphabetically by returned name before the comparison.
prompt:
Perform a read-only operations check for exactly two meeting records in the supplied sandbox: `Museum Partnership Briefing` in `Seattle` and `Fall Campaign Retrospective` in `Portland`. Use Pi's genuine shell tool to run the local `./meeting-registry` executable. Treat the executable as the sole supported interface to meeting records. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing data, its runtime audit directory, or anything under `.protected/`; do not manufacture, replay, or delete operation evidence.

In the first Pi shell-tool action that operates on the registry, start two separate exact meeting searches concurrently, one scoped to each requested city and name, and wait for both processes to finish. The searches must be distinct executable invocations launched by that same shell action. Do not combine the lookups into a broad search, search for any other record, or perform either requested search in a different action. Continue past this gate only if each exact search independently returns exactly one matching record with a nonempty stable meeting ID. If either lookup returns zero or multiple matches, lacks a stable ID, or returns a mismatched city or name, stop before every retrieval, do not create the comparison file, and identify the unresolved lookup in the final response. Never guess an ID.

Only after both search processes have completed and resolved uniquely, use a later, separate Pi shell-tool action to start two complete-record retrievals concurrently, one distinct executable invocation per meeting, and wait for both processes to finish. Each retrieval must use only the stable ID returned by its corresponding search. Do not start either retrieval while a search is running, retrieve another record, combine the IDs into one invocation, or derive status or date from search summaries, backing data, or any other source. Compare status and date only after both complete records have returned.

This check is strictly read-only. Do not update, cancel, notify, create, delete, archive, or otherwise mutate either requested meeting or any other record. Use no registry operation beyond the two exact searches and the two eligible complete-record retrievals. Do not alter managed state or execution evidence. Apart from runtime evidence created automatically by the executable, create only `meeting_comparison.md`, and leave no temporary coordination artifacts.

When both records resolve, write `meeting_comparison.md` as exactly three newline-terminated, nonblank Markdown bullet lines, with no heading, blank lines, or extra prose. List the two record bullets in ascending alphabetical order by the names returned in their complete records, then put the comparison bullet last. Format each record bullet exactly as `- <name> | <city> | ID <stable ID> | status <status> | date <date>`, preserving those five fields exactly as returned by its complete-record retrieval. Format the last bullet exactly as `- Comparison | statuses <match-or-differ> (<alphabetically-first status> vs <alphabetically-second status>) | dates <match-or-differ> (<alphabetically-first date> vs <alphabetically-second date>)`, replacing each `<match-or-differ>` with `match` only when the two corresponding returned values are equal and with `differ` otherwise. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. If it passes, return exactly the three bullet lines as your final response.
